sections.heading: Asymptomatic Limited Cutaneous Kaposi Sarcoma
sections.content:
Criteria: Patients with limited cutaneous disease defined as T0 (confined to skin and/or lymph nodes and/or minimal oral disease) and asymptomatic and cosmetically acceptable to the patient. For PWH, ART should be started or continued.
Options:
- Observation: Observe with ART optimization for PWH (Preferred, Category 2A)
Adjuvant logic: Not applicable.

sections.heading: Symptomatic or Cosmetically Bothersome Limited Cutaneous Kaposi Sarcoma
sections.content:
Criteria: Patients with limited cutaneous disease that is symptomatic or cosmetically bothersome, including PWH and those without HIV.
Options:
- Topical therapy: Alitretinoin 0.1% gel or imiquimod 5% cream (Other recommended, Category 2A)
- Radiation therapy: For localized lesions (Other recommended, Category 2A)
- Limited systemic therapy: 3–6 cycles for PWH initiating or re-initiating ART (Other recommended, Category 2A)
- Clinical trial (Other recommended, Category 2A)
Adjuvant logic: Not applicable.

sections.heading: Advanced Kaposi Sarcoma Eligible for Systemic Therapy
sections.content:
Criteria: Advanced disease defined as T1 (tumor-associated edema or ulceration, extensive oral KS, gastrointestinal KS, KS in organs other than lymph nodes) or extensive T0 cutaneous disease or nodal disease. Patient is eligible for systemic therapy based on performance status and absence of reversible contraindications.
Options:
- First-line systemic therapy: Liposomal doxorubicin for non-transplant KS; sirolimus for transplant KS (Preferred, Category 2A); linked regimen IDs: liposomal_doxorubicin, sirolimus, paclitaxel
- Clinical trial (Other recommended, Category 2A)
Adjuvant logic: Continue or initiate ART for PWH concurrently with systemic therapy.

sections.heading: Advanced Kaposi Sarcoma Not Immediately Eligible for Systemic Therapy
sections.content:
Criteria: Advanced disease with poor performance status or medical comorbidities that preclude upfront systemic therapy (e.g., active infection, high-level HIV viremia).
Options:
- Medical optimization: Address active infections, initiate/modify ART (Other recommended, Category 2A)
- Radiation therapy: For palliation or short-term management (Other recommended, Category 2A)
Adjuvant logic: Reassess eligibility for systemic therapy after optimization; if poor performance status is due to KS, systemic therapy may still be considered as it can improve performance status.

sections.heading: Relapsed/Refractory Limited Cutaneous Kaposi Sarcoma
sections.content:
Criteria: Relapsed or progressive disease after initial response, confirmed by biopsy to rule out mimickers.
Options:
- Repeat first-line systemic therapy: If previously effective and durable (Other recommended, Category 2A); linked regimen IDs: liposomal_doxorubicin, paclitaxel
- Alternative therapy: Alternative first-line systemic therapy or subsequent options (Other recommended, Category 2A); linked regimen IDs: nivolumab, pembrolizumab, pomalidomide
Adjuvant logic: Evaluate for inadequate HIV control and address possible change in ART with an HIV specialist.

nodes.label: Limited cutaneous KS, T0, asymptomatic, cosmetically acceptable. For PWH: start/continue ART.
Observe with ART optimization for PWH

flowcharts.title: Symptomatic or Cosmetically Bothersome Limited Cutaneous Kaposi Sarcoma

nodes.label: Limited cutaneous KS, symptomatic or cosmetically bothersome, any HIV status.
Topical therapy: alitretinoin 0.1% gel or imiquimod 5% cream
Limited systemic therapy (3–6 cycles) for PWH initiating/re-initiating ART

flowcharts.title: Advanced Kaposi Sarcoma Eligible for Systemic Therapy

nodes.label: Advanced KS (T1 or extensive T0/nodal), eligible for systemic therapy (good PS, no contraindications).
First-line systemic therapy: liposomal doxorubicin (non-transplant) or sirolimus (transplant)
Continue or initiate ART for PWH concurrently

flowcharts.title: Advanced Kaposi Sarcoma Not Immediately Eligible for Systemic Therapy

nodes.label: Advanced KS, poor PS or comorbidities precluding upfront systemic therapy.
Medical optimization: address infections, initiate/modify ART
Reassess eligibility for systemic therapy after optimization; consider systemic therapy if poor PS due to KS
Relapsed/progressive limited cutaneous KS after initial response (biopsy-confirmed).
Repeat first-line systemic therapy if previously effective and durable
Alternative first-line or subsequent options (nivolumab, pembrolizumab, pomalidomide)
Evaluate HIV control, consider ART change with specialist

edges.label: Other recommended, Category 2A if prior durable response
